modules.title: What is Umrah?
modules.body: Umrah is a visit to the Sacred House (the Ka'bah) that can be performed at any time of year. It consists of entering ihram, performing tawaf around the Ka'bah, walking sa'i between Safa and Marwah, and cutting the hair. Unlike Hajj, it is not tied to specific days. It is a deeply personal act of worship — take it at your own pace, and know that being unsure is normal.

modules.title: Ihram & its rules
modules.body: In the state of ihram, certain acts are set aside — such as cutting hair or nails, using scented products, and quarrelling. Enter ihram at the miqat with a sincere intention, and recite the Talbiyah often. For men, ihram is two unstitched white cloths; for women, ordinary modest dress. The point is simplicity and equality before Allah — everyone stands the same.

modules.title: The rites, start to finish
modules.body: A gentle map of the whole journey so nothing feels unfamiliar: (1) Enter ihram and make your intention. (2) Recite the Talbiyah on the way. (3) Tawaf — circle the Ka'bah seven times. (4) Pray two rak'ah near the Station of Ibrahim, then drink Zamzam. (5) Sa'i — walk seven times between Safa and Marwah. (6) Cut or trim the hair to leave ihram. That is Umrah, complete. The app will walk you through each one when you arrive.

modules.title: Etiquette in the Haram
modules.body: The Haram is crowded and shared by millions — patience is itself an act of worship. Keep your voice and heart calm, give way to the elderly and weak, and don't push toward the Black Stone if it means harming others. Keep your phone quiet and your gaze lowered. If you lose your place in a rite, don't panic — the app remembers where you are, and Allah knows your intention.
